title: ConformanceDecisionRequest
description: One Decider scenario on the behavioural-conformance wire (spec §6.3.1). A conformance runner reads a JSON ARRAY of requests on stdin and writes a JSON ARRAY of outcomes on stdout — one outcome per request, IN THE SAME ORDER — then exits 0; the array position is the only correlation, so ordering is load-bearing and there is no per-request id. For a decision request the runner folds `given` — a sequence of prior events — into fresh aggregate state (a clean slate per request; scenarios never share state), then decides the single `when` command against that state, answering with a ConformanceDecisionResponse. Every payload value on this wire is drawn from the WIRE SCALAR ALPHABET (§3.3): `boolean`, 64-bit signed `integer`, and `string` only; a field a payload schema declares as `number` or `date` (§3.2, §3.3) travels AS A STRING here, because the wire has no native number-with-fraction or date type. Self-contained: this schema shares no `$ref` across files and inlines every reusable shape in `$defs`.

given.description: The prior events the runner folds, in order, into fresh aggregate state before deciding `when`. May be empty (the empty history — decide against the initial state). Each item is an EventRef.

when.description: The single command decided against the folded state. A CommandRef.

scalar.description: A wire scalar (§3.3): the only value types that cross this wire. `boolean`, a 64-bit signed `integer`, or a `string`. A payload field declared `number` or `date` (§3.2) is carried here AS A STRING — the wire alphabet has no fractional-number or date type, so those cross as their string form.

eventRef.description: A reference to an event with its payload. EITHER a bare id string (shorthand for that event with the EMPTY payload) OR an object naming the `event` id and its `with` payload. A missing `with` is the empty payload. Payload values are wire scalars (§3.3).

oneOf.description: Bare event id — the event with an empty payload.

with.description: The event payload: field name → wire scalar. Absent means the empty payload.

commandRef.description: A reference to a command with its payload. EITHER a bare id string (shorthand for that command with the EMPTY payload) OR an object naming the `command` id and its `with` payload. A missing `with` is the empty payload. Payload values are wire scalars (§3.3).

oneOf.description: Bare command id — the command with an empty payload.

with.description: The command payload: field name → wire scalar. Absent means the empty payload.
